spec_match.rationale: Exercises :hover, :focus-visible, ::before chained after ::part() but only covers three of the many newly-allowed pseudos and omits ::selection, ::first-line, ::first-letter, :placeholder-shown which the spec also unblocks.

interactive.rationale: Three real toggle buttons drive a shadow-DOM custom element and emit live CSS — past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises interactive toggles with live application to a shadow-DOM custom element and the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: No coverage of what's still rejected — combinators, :has(), :nth-child(), ::part() chained after ::part(). The interesting failure modes are entirely absent.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195333643272192 in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables, toggle button overrides background AND color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the rejection set, not just the acceptance set
openQuestions.detail: The headline of this spec change is that 'most' pseudos now work but combinators and tree-structural pseudos still don't. The page should include a small list of selectors that remain rejected (e.g. ::part(x) > .y, ::part(x):has(.z), ::part(x):nth-child(2n), ::part(x)::part(y)) with a live CSS.supports() probe next to each so authors learn the boundary, not just the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add :focus-visible-vs-:focus distinction
openQuestions.detail: The current focus toggle only emits :focus-visible but the demo button can't actually keyboard-focus a shadow part without tabindex. Either wire tabindex onto the shadow button or surface the difference between :focus and :focus-visible — neither works in the same way and both are newly composable.

openQuestions.title: Surface ::selection and ::first-line in the live builder
openQuestions.detail: These are the headline pseudo-elements the spec change unblocks (the foundation-for-part-like-pseudos sibling demo lists them). Either add toggles for them here or explicitly hand off to that sibling page so a visitor lands on a complete coverage map.

summary: Minimal but honest live-toggle demo. Misses the chance to teach the rejection envelope (combinators, tree-structural pseudos, :has) which is the whole interesting story of the spec change. Expand the toggles or pair with a probe panel that distinguishes accepted from rejected chains.
